description: This map displays a false-colour infrared satellite image of an airport facility. The main airport area, including the dark grey runways, light grey/white buildings, and associated vegetated land, is outlined by a light blue polygon boundary. The number "1240", which corresponds to the CORINE Land Cover (CLC) code for 'Airports', is labelled within this outlined area. Surrounding the airport to the north and west are other built-up areas and agricultural fields, depicted in various shades of red, indicating vegetated areas. To the south and east, a winding river appears as a dark blue/black feature, flanked by riparian zones and further agricultural parcels. Additional land parcels are outlined by thinner, dark blue lines. No specific geographic location, scale bar, compass, or date is visible on the image itself, but the surrounding document context refers to examples such as Arlanda (Stockholm, Sweden) and airports in Germany.
